Title: High-quality sugar production by osgcs1 rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HAP2/GCS1 domain-containing sperm cell-specific fertilization protein with N-terminal signal sequence and C-terminal transmembrane domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsGCS1 causes fertilization failure but still permits pollen tube-dependent ovule enlargement, producing near-normal-sized watery seed-like structures with very high sucrose content and no embryo/endosperm.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major dietary sugar and biofuel feedstock
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; reciprocal crosses; pollen tube staining; histology; RNA-seq of ovules; LC-MS sugar quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGCS1 is a sperm cell-specific fertilization gene in rice. 2. Knockout lines show no pollen tube guidance defect but fail fertilization. 3. Mutant ovules enlarge into near-normal-sized watery seed-like tissues lacking embryo and endosperm. 4. The accumulated fluid is highly enriched in sucrose, indicating blocked sucrose-to-starch conversion after fertilization failure.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential entry point to engineer sugar-producing grains/crops by inducing fertilization failure coupled with continued sucrose unloading; may broaden sugar-crop resources, though sterility is a major constraint.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe reproductive failure/sterility; absence of viable seed production.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pollination and pollen tube content release are required; successful fertilization is absent in mutant.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies OsGCS1 as the main experimental line used for detailed analyses (g51) and links pollen tube content release to sucrose unloading, while fertilization is required for sucrose-to-starch conversion.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HAP2/GCS1 domain-containing sperm cell-specific protein lacking clear N-terminal signal sequence and with nearly absent transmembrane domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsGCS1-like independently causes a POEMed-like fertilization-defective watery seed-like phenotype similar to osgcs1.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major dietary sugar and biofuel feedstock
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; Tos17 mutant phenotyping; seed histology/observation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGCS1-like is a second rice GCS1 homolog expressed specifically in sperm cells. 2. Independent mutation produces watery seed-like tissues without embryo/endosperm. 3. This supports conservation of a fertilization-related role linked to sugar-rich POEMed-like ovules.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative target to induce sugary-grain phenotype, but associated sterility limits direct deployment.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Sterility and loss of normal grain formation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phenotype depends on pollination-associated ovule response.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although OsGCS1-like was validated phenotypically, the detailed transcriptome and sugar analyses focused mainly on osgcs1 line g51.
